sections.heading: Our Range of Market Research in Vietnam
sections.subtitle: Market research encompasses various types tailored to specific business goals, from brand research to customer segmentation. Understanding these facets helps companies make informed decisions and stay competitive. Let's explore some of these research methods.

items.title: Branch Research
items.desc: Brand research aims to develop or enhance company's brand to leave a strong, positive impact on your target market. It involves assessing brand awareness, customer loyalty, brand associations, and overall customer-brand relationships.

items.title: Marketing Campaign Evaluation
items.desc: Certain market research is geared towards assessing the effectiveness of marketing campaigns. It involves tracking metrics like online ad visibility and click-through rates. This data informs adjustments and guides future campaigns, ensuring efficient customer acquisition.

items.title: Competitor Research
items.desc: Competitor research provides insights for your company's strategic advantage. Analyzing competitors' marketing, brand, sales, and more reveals strengths and weaknesses to learn from.

items.title: Customer Segmentation Research
items.desc: Segmenting customers create distinct identities or segments within your wider consumer base. Knowing the many customer segments you wish to reach will assist you in developing customized advertising for each one.

items.title: Consumer Research
items.desc: Market research encompasses various types tailored to specific business goals, from brand research to customer segmentation. Understanding these facets helps companies make informed decisions and stay competitive.

items.title: Product Development Research
items.desc: Product development necessitates prior market research to validate the concept's value and continuous refinement over time. Monitoring sales trends and customer feedback can unveil shifting needs and inform product enhancements.

items.title: Usability Testing
items.desc: Usability testing, akin to product development research, centers on product functionality and user experience. For instance, launching a mobile app beta version allows user feedback for bug identification and issue resolution.

sections.heading: How to Conduct Market Research in Vietnam
sections.subtitle: Businesses employ diverse approaches to conduct market research. To start, distinguishing between primary and secondary research is vital for understanding data acquisition.

items.title: Primary Research
items.desc: Primary research entails data collection by a company or a designated expert for their market research. It includes exploratory research, which is open-ended, and research designed to address specific questions or issues.

items.title: Secondary Research
items.desc: Secondary research relies on existing data readily available for market research. It includes sources like government census data, industry reports, and market trend statistics, offering valuable insights into the broader market and competitors.

sections.heading: Methods of Primary Research in Vietnam
sections.subtitle: Numerous primary research methods are employed by companies to gain insights from consumers. Some of the most commonly used methods include:

items.title: Surveys
items.desc: Quick-response questionnaires gather feedback through multiple-choice or open-ended queries, accessible in various formats like in-person, phone, mail, or online, ensuring efficient data collection from a diverse audience.

items.title: Focus Groups
items.desc: Focus groups are smaller, led by a researcher to delve into consumer perspectives. Multiple groups are formed for comprehensive insights, with observation through one-way mirrors or recorded sessions.

items.title: Interviews
items.desc: Individual interviews, although more in-depth, lack the statistical reliability of larger surveys. However, they offer valuable insights from one customer, which can be beneficial for specific cases.

items.title: Observation
items.desc: Market observation entails discreetly watching consumers in natural settings, like a retail store, to analyze their behaviors. Researchers may examine security footage to identify shopping patterns and interactions with in-store displays.

items.title: Field Trials
items.desc: Field trials encompass implementing a business change on a limited scale and assessing outcomes. For instance, a restaurant chain may introduce a new menu item in a few locations to gauge its popularity before expanding it regionally.
